questions.question: Prove that every non-empty subset of the integers containing a smallest element has a least element. Use the Well Ordering Principle to justify your answer.
questions.hint: Refer to the Well Ordering Principle and its implications

questions.question: Consider the sequence of integers: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ... . Using the Well Ordering Principle and mathematical induction, prove that the nth term of this sequence is given by 2^(n-1).
questions.hint: Use the definition of the sequence and mathematical induction

questions.question: Prove that the set of positive integers is well-ordered using the Well Ordering Principle. Then, use mathematical induction to prove that the sum of the first n positive integers is given by n(n+1)/2.
questions.hint: Refer to the definition of well-ordered sets and mathematical induction
